message:
fix(runtime): replace panicking inner() with fallible try_inner() on RuntimeHandle

RuntimeHandle::inner() called .expect() when upgrading a Weak reference,
which panics if the runtime has already been dropped. This is a real
scenario when a stale weak handle outlives its runtime (e.g. in shutdown
callbacks or cross-runtime references).

Changes:
- Replace inner() with try_inner() -> Result<Arc<RuntimeInner>, SpawnError>
- Add SpawnError::RuntimeUnavailable variant for the upgrade-failure case
- spawn() still panics (documented contract) but now via .expect() on
  try_spawn(), keeping the panic message clear
- try_spawn() propagates RuntimeUnavailable to callers
- spawn_blocking() and blocking_handle() return None on stale handles
  instead of panicking, matching their Option return types
- Update doc comments to describe the stale-handle behavior
- Add test verifying weak handle returns SpawnError::RuntimeUnavailable
  after runtime drop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
